groups.group_label: Complete 1 semester units from the following

courses.title: Introduction to Motion Picture and Video Production

notes: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .

This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. However, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division courses as possible from the " Recommended Preparation " section below to support timely degree completion. Students applying to non-screening majors are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements prior to transfer. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.

PREPARATION FOR THE MAJOR Although not required for admission, transfer students are encouraged to complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, prior to transferring: FILM 20A – Introduction to Film Studies ONE lower-division Film and Digital Media course Refer to the "Recommended Preparation" and "Additional Major Preparation Courses" sections below for lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements. A maximum of two courses from another institution may be substituted if articulated through assist.org or approved by Film and Digital Media faculty. At least one of the major qualification courses must be taken at UCSC. FILM 20P cannot be used toward declaring the major. Additional Information for Transfer Transfer students can finish the Film and Digital Media general major requirements within two years. While not required for admission, students are encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult with your community college counselor. Integrated Critical Practice Concentration In addition to the general Film and Digital Media major, students may choose to pursue the Integrated Critical Practice Concentration (ICPC) . This concentration offers a more rigorous pathway, tailored for seniors with exceptional abilities in both critical studies and production. It provides exclusive classes designed for those who aim to integrate creative and scholarly work at an advanced level. Students are eligible to apply for the concentration in the last quarter of their junior year. Please visit the Film & Digital Media Department website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Film and Digital Media B.A. Program Statement
